shared_properties: Timer glyphs keep a strict 8px cap height to match the Press Start 2P HUD scale in the GDD.
Digits remain monochrome so time readout does not overpower gameplay art.
Each glyph cell preserves consistent side bearings for stable MM:SS composition.
differences: Atlas is 88x8px with glyph cells for numerals 0 through 9 plus a colon.
The glyph set is optimized for right-aligned timer layout at the top-right safe area.
Colon spacing is narrower than numeral spacing so the MM:SS grouping stays compact.

context.usage: Monospaced bitmap digits used to render the survival timer in MM:SS format.

shape.silhouette: Blocky 8px HUD glyphs with square corners and open counters.
shape.collision_read: UI-only asset with no collision meaning.

acceptance_criteria: Atlas size is exactly 88x8px with eleven 8x8px glyph cells.
Digits compose a readable MM:SS timer within a 40px-wide live HUD string.
The colon glyph remains legible at 1x scale and does not collapse into the numeral stems.

reference_images.label: slip_down_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds HUD contrast, muted dungeon palette, and low-saturation screen overlays for the active widget.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: Texture-atlas-backed UI glyph rendering for a screen-space timer node.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load through AssetServer during the Loading state and store the atlas in a timer HUD resource.
runtime_notes: Use Bevy 0.13 with ImagePlugin::default_nearest() so every asset stays pixel-perfect at 4x upscale.
Compose MM:SS from atlas indices instead of rasterizing dynamic text at runtime.
Right-align the timer node to whole pixels so digit spacing stays crisp.
